review_text: Unfortunately room wasn’t like it looked in photos, mine has a main back road within 5-6m could hear cars bikes most the night , could hear neighbours on the other side of adjoining cabin all night . Bathroom had mould and mites on the ceiling, Exhaust fan in bathroom sounded like a rocket had to close door to hear tv which was also in adjoining room to bed room as was A/C. . Cold vinyl flooring and Lounge was that close to tv you got a saw neck looking up to watch it  . Unfinished flooring , pieces missing . Cabin had broken cladding on it . Neighbours had the up car parked on grass next to my lounge room . Gym had broken equipment . Unfortunately couldn’t recommend it at at

Google Review for Ingenia Holiday Parks Ulladulla

⭐️⭐️

We are very disappointed with our stay at Ingenia Holiday Parks Ulladulla. We booked two cabins for 4 adults, 4 children, and 2 dogs, primarily because it was advertised as pet-friendly. However, we were shocked to find that pets were not allowed anywhere on the premises!

We also faced issues with basic amenities; fresh towels were never provided during our three-night stay. We asked and were told they couldn't provide. Our main reason for choosing this park was the water slides, which we learned were only open from 12-1 PM on Friday and Saturday. This meant our kids missed out on that experience entirely.

The accommodations were really basic and quite cold; there were no spare blankets available to keep warm. To top it off, the children and pets discovered ant poison pods in our cabins, which posed a significant safety risk for both our pets and children.

Overall, we felt very let down, especially considering the high price we paid per cabin. We expected a much better experience and would not recommend this park based on our experience.

We love staying here.
First time camping as normally we have a cabin and an overall a great experience.
The only thing I’d comment on is the fact the games room is never locked and given we had a site directly across, inconsiderate adults would often be heard in the games room well after closing (sometimes 1am ) and children accessing it well before the 9am opening.
Both being an inconvenience while trying to sleep.
Also maybe a sign near the bbqs as some people use it and don’t even bother to clean it.
We stayed at a camp site. Location is great. The water slides are fun for the kids. The arcade room is fun for big kids. The staff are very welcoming and go.out of their way to ensure your comfort.
Always great stay but getting more expensive over Easter

I stayed here for two nights recently, in a cabin, while my Motorhome was having some repairs done.
I had travelled through an area with no mobile reception to get there and had broken down earlier in the day but arrived about 5 minutes after the office closed. The information received prior to my arrival contained no mention of what happens if you arrive late.
I had several texts, emails and messages, from the office, requesting me to attend the office on my arrival to pay for my accommodation despite having already paid when I booked. These I never received until I arrived at the office. However there was one text I did not get - until the middle of the night; the one that tells what to do if you arrive late. After ringing the emergency number and waiting half an hour for a reply, I discovered the code for the late arrivals box to retrieve my key and cabin number.
On entering the cabin I find the windows all open, the ceiling fan going and the air conditioner running and set to 19°c. Clearly a very costly way to remove the stale smell of the cabin. The bed cover smelt dusty, the floor felt dusty underfoot, the lounge was shabby and the shower was filthy (see photos).
On attending the office the next morning, a little out of breath from walking back from the repairer, I was spoken to rather curtly by the woman on reception because I hadn’t answered my phone the previous day - a difficult task with no reception to either receive or answer calls, texts or emails. When I asked for a Senior’s Discount on my accommodation, I was curtly told “no”.
I found the accommodation expensive, especially considering the lack of cleanliness. I guess the high cost has to cover the electricity bill for running the air conditioning at 19°c with the windows open on a warm day. Perhaps if they paid the cleaning staff more to do a better job they wouldn’t need to have the windows open and the air conditioner running to fake the freshness.
Needless to say, my negative experience means I will not stay here again, nor will I recommend it to my fellow grey nomads.

Had a fantastic one week stay here waiting for our car to be repaired. Very fair pricing, amenities clean. Lots of entertainment including tennis, table tennis, gym and sauna. Short walk into Ulladulla.

We stayed here one night for the kids to use the great water park facilities.

The park itself is large and has various areas but the facilities are either very good or out of service completely.

Lots of dirt on the grassed site.

Overall it's a nice park, but aside from the water park and cheap prices, I cannot see why people would return to this park when compared with others.
Loved the park and all its amenities the lady on reception was a gem

Best caravan park in have been to in a long time, neat, clean, affordable, staff were very helpful. i showered without my flip flops for the first time in a while as the shower was pristine. even has a sauna! definitely will be back 10 out of 10

After a medical emergency the staff could not have been more helpful, which made things so much easier for me.
The only thing I could fault is the tight space
Around your  van & car.
Found facilities to be extremely clean.

,

Bit dated, but facilities are well maintained and overall clean

Kids were mad for the waterslides
Nice van park but facilities could be cleaner and should be cleaned on the weekend.

Amazing. We have recently booked a big family affair and the staff - AMAZING !! Sophie - what a trooper - swaps, changes, nothing was a bother.

The Park - perfect ! Who doesn’t love a water slide 😍😍. Bbq facilities - top notch. Undercover, large groups.

Love to see a pickleball court mapped out in the tennis court 🙏🙏

Very run down in major need of renovation, the caravan sites are terrible our van was hanging over and all site are pretty much down hill , apart from being in a good location that all the good I can only say , extremely overpriced we won’t be back
Lovely spot to bring the family and dog. Park and amenities were clean and staff were very friendly! Kids loved the slides. Easy walk to racecourse beach and ex-servos club too!

Ingenia Holidays Ulladulla: STEER CLEAR

We stayed at Ingenia Holidays Ulladulla as part of a large group, and we understand that when accommodating many guests, especially at a busy time of year, things can’t always be perfect. That said, given how busy the park was and the amount we paid, we genuinely expected a much higher standard than what was delivered. We all caravan regularly and have stayed at many parks before in our large group, and have never had this many issues.

• Facilities: Pools and splash parks were dirty and poorly maintained, with the slide closing by 5 pm or earlier each day. Water slides were not monitored appropriately, which raised safety concerns. None of the facilities opened before 9 am, including the gym and sauna, which was disappointing for guests wanting to use them earlier in the day.

• Amenities: Toilets and showers were not cleaned regularly, were filthy, and often had an unbearable smell, making them very unpleasant to use. Not to mention the poor ventilation which on a day in the middle of January is not ideal.

• Caravan Sites: Despite requesting large sites for our big caravans and tow rigs, the sites were extremely tight. Non-concrete sites were uneven and had large potholes, which our kids repeatedly fell into. Grass had died, or was full of bindis.

• Laundry: There were only two washing machines and dryers servicing a very large area with many guests, meaning we had to do laundry in the middle of the night.

• Safety: Speed limits were consistently ignored, and there were no speed bumps to slow traffic. We made several complaints due to serious safety concerns, including a child being bumped by a car while riding their bike (not part of our group). Management were contacted, however the only response was a text message and no further action was taken.
• Security & Staffing: a large side gate was always left open, allowing anyone to walk in at any time. There appeared to be no staff present or monitoring the park for most of the weekend, it genuinely felt like we were there on our own.

• Noise: Bins were collected before 6 am each morning, taking around 20 minutes and waking children daily, which is not ideal on holidays. The bins are located on the side fence which backs on to all of the caravan sites.

Overall, the park felt neglected, unsafe, and poorly managed. Given the cost and peak holiday period, we expected far more. We would not stay again unless significant improvements are made. Please consider all of this when booking, especially with children. The safety risks are insane and we did not relax all week. Genuinely not saying this to just whinge, but to let other families know before making the same mistakes we have.

The park is very neglected, unsafe and poorly managed. Sites are small and uneven and many have pot holes (we stayed in an ensuite site and it wasn’t good at all). Pool is small, spas and sauna felt icky and with no boom gate anyone could come and go as they please. Would not recommend
Fantastic stay, great park and the kids loved the slides and pools! Highly recommended!

Lots of positives about this park - clean, great amenities including 2 water slides, 2 pools, 2 heated spas, sauna, gym, library, squash court, camp kitchen, fire pit and games room. ||We were greeted at check in warmly and informative about the local area and recommendations free flowing. Lots of walking tracks available with simply stunning scenery. ||A few big negatives that we faced were the size of the sites (quite a number unable to fit a van with rollout and car), the pure number of sites in a smallish area (think can of sardines) and the all day and all night noise from the highway. After a sleepless first night we purchased ear plugs from Bunnings and ended up shutting all windows in fan and running the AC. ||Overall an enjoyable stay but be prepared for road noise and know that you will be very very close to your neighbour.||Sites 53-58 offer the most space as well as a grassed area.

We have just recently finished our two week stay at this park using their powered sites for our 21ft caravan.

For the money you pay to stay here particularly in the peak periods is disappointing. The park is in need of some TLC particularly with the toilets / bathroom facility. There is zero ventilation, not even a ceiling fan. So when the showers are being used it becomes uncomfortably hot and stuffy. Subsequently due to a lack of ventilation the ceiling has clear areas of mould. The “fans” above the toilet area do nothing due to the extravagant amount of dust as per pictures attached.

I think it’s also worth noting for anyone who chooses this park for a caravan site, some sites have a huge slope which we weren’t informed about upon booking. So can lead to some people being unprepared.

Some of the caravan sites and camping sites are directly adjacent to multiple large bins. Most of which through out the hot days were open and the smell travelled through the park.

This was our first camping experience, and we chose Ingenia Ulladulla for a special holiday together. Although we had an unpleasant experience on the first night, the staff were incredibly kind, patient, and understanding when we spoke to them the next day. Sophie and her colleagues showed genuine care and professionalism, which completely changed our stay and allowed us to truly enjoy the rest of our time. We especially loved the cabin area, which was much quieter and more relaxing. We are very grateful to the Ulladulla team and would love to come back again in the future. 🏕️

Great location and the welcome pack for the kids at checkin was a nice touch however that's where it stops. Facilities are filthy! With the park full over xmas they were cleaned once in the morning. Even when cleaned it was far from sanitary. We were also packed in like sardines considering the price we paid to not be able to have room for our car on our site is not ok!

The cabin we stayed in was tired and a bit run down. Not the cleanest. The walls were all grotty and the cabin not cleaned that well. The versndah gate was almost rotten. Looked like someone had vomited on the grass right next to the stairs to get into the cabin. Not a nice way to kick off our weekend away. You think the cleaners would have at lest thrown a bucket of water of the puke prior to signing off it as "cleaned". The cabin had a strange not so pleasant smell to it. Staff were all friendly. Kids pool didn't look that clean.

I didn’t actually stay in a place here but just wanted to mention that the people in charge are incredibly genuine. When booking through Airbnb I accidentally clicked on a place here instead of the one I was originally going to. Despite my mistake they were more than happy to refund my booking which shows great character and generosity

I recently stayed at Ingenia Holiday Park Ulladulla and wanted to share something that may be helpful for future guests, particularly those driving MPV-style family cars.

The designated parking bay for my cabin (C28) had an unusually high concrete edge on one side. It was difficult to see when arriving in the evening due to low lighting, and unfortunately my car’s underside was damaged when reversing in. After measuring it the next morning, the height was significantly higher than what you’d normally expect in a parking space.

I reported the issue to park staff, who were polite and took photos. After ~10 days and receiving no response from Ingenia management, my insurer contacted Ingenia directly. Ultimately the park did not accept liability, leaving me to cover a $900 insurance excess for necessary repairs.

I’m sharing this not to place blame, but to warn other travellers to take extra care when parking at the cabin bays, especially at night, as the raised concrete isn’t obvious until you’re right on top of it. The rest of the park was pleasant, and I’m hopeful that management may review the lighting or marking of that bay to prevent similar incidents for future guests.

If Ingenia is able to reassess the situation or provide a resolution, I’m more than happy to update this review.
We stayed in a single cabin, the bed was incredibly comfortable. Great location and facilities.

Affordable power camping. Facilities are clean and in great condition. Hot showers with great pressure. Affordable laundry facilities. And we really enjoyed the gym. We got a key for after hours use 💪

Dog friendly (keep on lead!)

Reception was friendly and we were really happy with our stay.

All of these issues were raised whilst staying at the park. Once raised, I was ignored. We left a day early.
- The floors were notably sticky under foot. This was discovered to be as a result of a dirt build up that could only be described as disgusting. See attached photo of a baby wipe used on ONE piece of timber floor. No more than a 30x30cm piece of flooring.
- Noted the aircon was in working condition, however see attached image of filters. This is the air my family is breathing in. These have no been serviced in months and months.
- Upon arrival, we found 3 items left in the cabin from any previous owner. A baby bottle full of unknown liquid, a pair of socks and a toy small enough to present a choking hazard. Having a small child who would find the bottle and drink it, or eat the toy, this is highly dangerous and in appropriate.
- We tried to use the spa area, to find a code on the gate. Managed to get the code, to find the spa broken awaiting repair. Then tried to use the spa at 10:10, to find it still locked. All other facilities were locked at whatever time they found convenient as opposed to what was advertised. The grassed areas were covered in bindis causing issues for kids without enclosed footwear.
- After 3 nights at the park we wished to change the towels and were told that was no possible due to there not being enough and to try again the following day after the delivery.

As stated, we left a night early and despite raising these issues, were ignored. Wouldn’t return.
Great amenities and location was convenient for us.

Horrible Highway Kids park
The park is nowhere near a beach, would need to cross the noisy highway to go anywhere. Staff not welcoming and very unhelpful. The park was empty, but the receptionist could only offer incompatible sites for our rig. We got the message they only want young families there so we ended up not staying and going elsewhere, despite having already paid for 3 days.

We stayed in a 2 bedroom deluxe villa, which was an excellent and roomy cabin. The park facilities were very well maintained s and the staff was exceptionally nice!
The camping site is a bit of a wind-channel and it would have been wonderful to have some trees for shade there. The cabins were located in a lovely green area. 2 camp kitchens spread out the holiday makes nicely and the fire pit was fabulous.
The water slides were a full success for our small to medium kids.
Not so great was that the sauna, which was advertised as 18+ location, was constantly blocked by children or teenagers, which is clearly not a park issue but rather the lack of parent supervision/ taking rules seriously. No biggie, though.
Did I mention that there was a full squash court? Yass!

What a change in experience we stayed in another inginia location night before and that was awful. This however is the complete opposite as a family of 4 we had to share the same room witch was actually nice for a change. The cabin was nice and clean and I am a sticker for clean. Under the sofas and beds where very clean this made me very happy as it proves a great turn over service unlike last resort. The heater worked so we where kept nice and toasty on a cold night. The cabin was very well presented. Although sleeping in same room for some maybe a big no no for some we actually really enjoyed it as we all went to bed early and we read bedtime stories to the kids in bed. The utilitys are clean and their is a lot on site for kids and family. Although the water slides are shut due to season it all looks fab. The only downside is the games room as had many coin operated machines that didn't work. Although that's probably an outside company we did lose a few dollars. Other than that very happy

We had a really disappointing stay at this holiday park and honestly wouldn’t recommend it to anyone. The toilets were absolutely disgusting, and since our site was right next to them, we had to deal with the horrible smell all day long. It completely ruined the experience. I have to say that the cleaning girls did their best, and you could see they were trying to keep things tidy, but it’s clear the problem is everything around the toilet area , the smell, the maintenance, and the poor design. It was unbearable.

The park also has no security at all , it’s completely open, which made us feel uncomfortable, especially at night. One of the BBQ areas doesn’t even have lighting, so you can’t use it after sunset, which is ridiculous for a place meant for outdoor activities.
Maybe if you have small kids you’ll find something to do, but if you’re looking for a peaceful or comfortable stay, don’t bother.

Really enjoyed meeting the other guests wonderful ! Just the best a big thankyou to Tenelle hope i got the spelling right Robyn and Debbie and Dot  the best customer service at a very uncertain time for me after a relocation and the death of one of my beloved cats while at the park thankyou julie tinkabell and Bob the cat 🐈

Winter stop over, cheap powered sites in the off season, we stayed 2 nights on our way north, the caravan sites are tight, but not impossible to get into. The caravan sites are close to the children’s playground which was super fun for the little ones, heated pool, spas and splash park, the water slides weren’t operational during our stay, but hey it’s winter! Multiple activities, playgrounds, pools and spas for all ages.

This is an ultra budget caravan park/cabin resort.

Nothing flash, nothing special. Good for the kids with the play ground down the bottom and the water park.

We booked the 6 person cabin. 1 adult 2 kids. There was an issue when we got there as the booking online default is 2 adults. The receptionist, Robyn, had an issue and had to charge me a pretty steep extra fee because it was 2 kids rather than 2 adults.

Ended up making this a rip off and not a good experience. 6 person cabin. Give me a break.

The room wouldn’t even fit 6 people as advertised.

We absolutely loved the cabin  Clean and tidy. Very nice layout of the cabin too. The amenities were amazing. I think Michelle at reception served us (prior booking reccomended for ease), she was super amazing. Thank you Michelle. Out kid loved the slide and heated pool, there is a entertainment room, small gym, tennis and basketball court, suna and a library. Highly recommended to stop here for a night or a few. Not forgetting, there are bbq areas just outside the cabin. 5 stars all the way guys! Keep rocking. We shall visit again soon.

Been numerous times and always enjoyed my stay. Sooo many amenities for the price. Very friendly staff.
And sooo many amenities! Pools, slides, hot tubs, gym, sauna, tennis and basketball courts... you should visit. 👌

A bit noisy.
Very cool waterslides and pool for kids!

The kids loved the pool, slides and games room. The cabin was clean and tidy and well serviced, beds were comfortable.  The grounds were well kept and staff were helpful and very friendly.  Far enough from the main road so there was no traffic noise

we stayed in site 54 a large grass site which was fantastic as we have a little dog and like to sent up a fenced area for him.
The amenities here are outstanding and for families there is so much for them to do in the park.
Great family getaway quiet and slides were the highlught
We stayed here overnight in our 9m bus with our 2 dogs and it was fantastic.
The staff were so lovely and really enjoyed the water slides.The cabin was so clean and spacious. Had a fab time!

Having been travelling for 3 months this was a lovely park with friendly staff and great for kids but the amenities were the worst I've seen regarding cleanliness! The bins were constantly overflowing with paper towels, often no toilet paper, the sanitary bins were overflowing, the floor was always wet and dirty and water was dripping from the mouldy ceiling with condensation (no ventilation) when showering! There is a sign outside to mop after your shower, well hard to do that with no mop! I saw a young cleaner who said they clean once a day and said the mens is worse which my husband agreed with! Well in school holidays with 3 or 4 toilets and showers once a day is certainly not enough! The adult spa room looked great so stepping into the water I couldn't work out what to press for bubbles so rang reception who advised they don't work  due to rain maybe upsetting it and a plumber was coming? A sign on the door would have been handy! In the camp kitchen the drinks and nibbles machine was totally empty! The electric car station was out of order. If your site is close to the road that's good for bins but if over other side no bins unless you walk to the road or go to camp kitchen! I'm not normally one to complain but these amenities really were a health hazard! Lucky nobody has slipped over!
Was better before now is crap 5 stars before now has to many rules

Park was clean, but unfortunately the park does not give much space to be able to maneuver caravans into spots, also some caravans were protruding onto the driveways with room at the back of vans, some caravans also had reversed too far back going into our site, the site markings were not very visible with grass growing over more than half of the concrete marks, a lot of sites had their guy ropes going onto adjoining sites. Toilets near the pool and slides had throw up over the sinks and stains all over a toilet seat.
Staff were friendly and helpful.

Stayed here recently. Busy period. Layout of caravan area pretty tight if you are someone with a big unit ensure you call ahead and make sure you are in an easier to access area, facilities good but not cleaned out frequently enough given the number of people. Showers also the crappy jayco/pre built type with no shelves or anything for clothes and not cleaned often enough.
Most grasses sites are now dirt patch sites when you park your van, which I understand - maybe time for some synthetic grass pads to be installed.
Unfortunately for us during our stay we had fools moving into the kitchen gaming area after 10pm(silent time) causing noise and chaos with their behaviour staying in sitescloseby. This made it harder with young kids trying to sleep. Luckily the park did get involved and asked them to move back to their cabins so credit to that. The highway is also right along it so if you are a sensitive sleeper best avoid because the trucks hurl past.
Lots of traffic noise, right on main road, excellent facilities

the positive factors of our recent stay was very friendly staff in office.|the cabin was very well set out, with a full kitchen, 3 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms.|the linen was really good, towels & sheets.|the negative factors of the cabin was that it was not clean, there was mold on both bathroom roofs, which was very distressing as my grandson especially is very allergic to mold.|all the windows were dirty & once again very moldy.|window screens either had holes in them or were torn away from the frame, causing not been able to open windows or doors.|my son in law had to clean the pedestal fan as it was filthy so that we could get some sort of air flow in the bedroom that i slept in.|in two of the bedrooms there were steel double bunks which we had to make up ourselves, having to make these double bunks up ourselves really wasn't acceptable in itself but it was really difficult to make them up. we had to pull bunk beds out to get behind them to try & tuck the sheets into the mattress, upon doing that the floors obviously hadn't been cleaned.|there were numerous issues with maintenance problems, the front sliding door latch was very loose, probably needed to be replaced, kitchen cupboard door in corner would not close, deck off to side look as though it was rotted & therefore we didn't go out on it.|the big water slide on our first day was not operational due to staff member being unwell, the second day instead of slide opening at advertised 10am we were informed it would open at 12noon.|i have now stayed at two ingenia holiday places & have found issues with both places. very disappointing, especially for my family who travelled from coffs harbour.

This park is amazing for families with young kids ,however you are packed in very tight ,no shade ,it is a dog  friendly park and that's fine but pet owners need to be responsible and consider other guest when their dogs are barking early morning or late at night ,our site was dirt based so trying to avoid the dirt we moved our van over ,which meant we could not put the awning out otherwise a great park for kids

Too close to main highway so found it noisy with frequent heavy trucks passing. Caravan sites are on the small side. We were unable to park next to our caravan of average size. The whole camping/caravan area is also small with no shade. Pool is also small  and narrow with many vehicles caravans etc directly passing the water play area where it gets very congested with campers and their vehicles some protruding out next to the passing vehicles where kids are playing. Other parking is available if you are there in time to get a space. Obviously weekends are the worst, mid week had much more room to move but still noisy.

My kid had so much fun at the playground and waterslide. It does feel noisy during the night cuz the A1 if you at the power site .The weather  is unpredictable you will need to be prepared for the strong wind and the rain .Amenities is convenient n staff are friendly and helpful :)

Super happy with our stay at Ingenia Ulladulla. We booked this last minute and loved that it had water slides.

We booked a powered site that fit our camper trailer and car well. Amenities were good considering it was a busy long weekend. Our son loved the pool and water slides the most.
It is close to the main area of Ulladulla and Burrill Lake.
Overall our stay was fantastic. I’d read the reviews after we booked and it’s very mixed however we didn’t encounter any issues. The only thing I would say would be that the spa and sauna facilities while a great thing to have there was filled with kids. Not a relaxing space and the sauna had broken during our stay which was a shame.

We will however definitely be back.

Wouldnt stay here again. Facilities looked good but paid a fortune and used none due to poor weather and high noise levels. Was impossible to park our van on a ridiculous shaped shoebox site with a big drop and site streets were a carpark with other guests blocking everybody else. The stress of trying to park and set up and watch our kids on the busy one way streets was definitely not worth the amount we paid. Surrounding traffic to the park being a main road added alot of noise also.

This was not what we thought. It was very over priced, the pool was not nice, very disappointed with the park as a whole, be very careful as there are kids everywhere and not taking any notice of cars and towing vehicles. Such a shame.

Went on the water slides with my kids, expecting a fun afternoon. Instead, the "fast" slide delivered an unexpected and unwelcome surprise: tears in my swimming vest and multiple lacerations on my back

Left early, would not stay again. Camping area like a carpark, small sites, no trees or character to any site. Facilities overall good, fun for the kids but very limited opening times for water slides, pool, tennis court etc even in summer holidays. Games room just arcade games needing coins. Lots of traffic noise. Can’t walk to beach (roads tricky to cross). Don’t expect to use the fire pit between Oct and Mar regardless of the conditions. Overall we were disappointed.
Wonderful holiday place for kids and families!!!!
I have stayed at lots of places and this was terrible. It does not look like the nice photos when you get there. Pool was gross full of ducks and poo. Never cleaned. Don't stay

My focus on parks largely circles around how much enjoyment my kids have. They had a great time, the pools slides and splash park all a hit (although splash park is infant focused).
We had relatives in a cabin which was fairly priced and well maintained, we had an ensuite site which was not as good. They are tight sites, and we knew our larger van was at the limit of size but the bookings allowing nearly 9m travel length should in my opinion, be more like 7m.

Go the powered site instead, more room and better grass coverage (at time of writing).

Would I stay here again? Yes, I would. But I'd stick to powered site. The bonus though, I didn't have impacts of road noise from the highway it's adjacent to, so the wall fencing does reduce the sound well.

The cabins are comfortable and are equipped with the basics for a short holiday. Ulladulla is a very picturesque coastal township. I would like to find a hairdryer, a sharp knife, and perhaps some cling wrap for guests. Beverley is a great host/owner.

Good value for money. Amazing waterslides, playgrounds and pools (multiple of each!). Cabins are well appointed with very comfortable beds.
It would be great if there was a little bit more to do for kids. Maybe scheduled activities or a self-paced treasure hunt etc.
Nothing walkable from the holiday park and no cafe/restaurant on site.
Schöner Campingplatz und sehr saubere Duschen, Toiletten und Laundry. Das Personal war nett, hilfsbereit und freundlich .
Placed in very crowded area, no room to put awning up due to space. Noisy during the day. Facilities very clean and updated.

Lovely caravan park with great staff. Camp kitchen and other facilities are top notch. Great pool/waterslide kept the kids well amused.
One small improvement point, please take care of the bindis.
In summary: Fantastic park, we will defiantly stay here next time we're passing through Ulladulla.

I booked a weekend away here because friends were going and said it was awesome last time. This time we arrived and the kids just wanted the slide. They put it on for two hours of the day. It’s spring and the whole reason we came was because of the slides. Don’t recommend it’s false advertising unless you don’t care about the water slides.
Great family oriented park. Stayed in a nicely appointed unit. Comfortable bed and pillows. Shower large with good water pressure.

The park is small only 4 small streets. The Grass is absolutely covered in bindi, if you don't have a floor mat you might have some issues.

The sites are small and hard to reverse a caravan into due to the very narrow streets, luckily we had no one across from us so we could straighten out. You can't park your car on your site, you either need to park on a spare site or in the car park. The sites themselves are all rolling down hill so you'll need to have levelling ramps for your van in order to be comfortable.

Bathrooms were good over all, I had to do a lap looking for bins as they weren't in the locations noted on the map given.

The pool area needs a lot of maintenance, there are tiles missing from walls, cracked tiles around the pool, the water slide wasn't running but looks like it was very tired.

Was clean and tidy.  Customer service was great. Had everything you need . Cook top and fridge clean and tidy. Bed is soft but comfortable.  TV reception wasn't great.
Great for families,  lots of things for the kids to do. Nice BBQ areas.
Only issue for smokers no smoking in the grounds so need to go out to the road to smoke.
Would recommend.
Beautiful family camping place. Secure n safe for kids. Pet friendly. Pool, souna is relaxing.
The bunk beds are as hard as wood because they have a wooden base under the mattress.
Stopped over for the night, great campsite and loved the medieval castle theme.

Waterslides, playgrounds, and pool are great for kids. Waterslides are not always open by seems of it. Our first day they were with end of Easter traffic, but our 2nd day they were not which disappointed the kids greatly as we planned our stay so it wouldn't be as busy on activities as Easter folks had left.  Oh well. Amenities were nice enough, small laundry line is wedged in corner next to dump point...so if you are doing laundry like our plan was for this stop that might be something to note.  Park easy to get to, right off main road. Spaces are VERY tight and small.  So if it's a busy period you better be 100% confident in your backing skills if you have a bigger caravan. Overall great spot for a short stay, staff were very friendly. Playground that's better for little kids is tucked behind cabins (pirate ship design) ;)

The sites (we had a slab site) are really squishy; we couldn’t fit our car on our site at all. Upon arrive our neighbors had to move there car just so we could get in. |Site sizes should be available on the website so people know before booking. The person I spoke to on the phone said we’d have no problems but it was tight. ||Great water slides and pool ( the pool up the back with cabins is much quieter if your looking for more relaxing vibe) |Amenities were tidy but could have use more regular attention. ||Overall an ok park but definitely all on top of one another and the noise from both main road was very loud. |Not a park we will visit again unfortunately.

Excellent customer service from Robyn. We stayed overnight on a last minute trip. We got a small cabin, great value for money.
The park itself is very clean and set out really well. There are plenty of bbq/kitchen areas scattered around the park making easy access for all guests.
They have 2 pools, water slides, plunge pools, heated spas and sauna, gym, kids playgrounds, games room and a fenced off fire pit area.

Staff were helpful, very polite, quiet and efficient.
As a last minute booking we were very grateful to be given a camp site.
Fantastic for the family

The cabin was well laid out, immaculately clean and perfect for a seven night stay. |The camp site has all the amenities, which are kept clean and well stocked. There is an excellent camp kitchen which was great for family meals. The electric barbecues were plentiful around the park. The children loved the pools, water slides and play areas. It is also in a great spot for exploring the local area. We will definitely be booking again for next year

Blown away by value for money!
Staff was friendly, room was nice and very clean and there was so much to do at the park.

Not a good tent camping experience
Tent site provided was a low lying dirt patch, #9. So when it rained flowed under tent. Mud.
That site be being right by Highway kept us awake at night - loud traffic.
Struggled to find a toilet with soap which I did me mention to reception.
Kids liked the waterslide
The nice part of town was the cabins area
Tennis court was also good although you can’t book a time slot - just wait around till free.
It was good to get away with the kids but happy to leave !
Perfect for the kids over New Years eve. All the families were lovely and the kids played nicely together.
Sites are to small, amenities are very filthy needs cleaning more often

The caravan park is located in what feels like an industrial area. We stayed from 27th of December, so understandably it is an extremely busy time of year, but sites are VERY small and we had neighbouring sites parking their cars into and onto our site. The toilets located closest to us were often dirty, without soap or toilet paper. The toilet/shower block had black mould all over the ceilings - photos taken. We left our booking 2 nights early as it was far too busy, squished in like sardines and unclean facilities.
My firat time camping exoerience at ingenia holidays ulladulla. It has been a memorable holiday experience.  Camping sites are really spaceous and neat.
Cleam and well maintained place but spa was too small for all people. But kids enjoyed the water slides and everything. Love the tennia court!

Only stayed 1 night and Upon arrival got text to say waterslides closed for afternoon. Found out next day was for staff Xmas party. Ourselves and many others very disappointed, only booked this park so young kids could use the slides. Management were extremely rude and ignorant when approached about the issue.
Should have been notified of Scheduled slide closure at time of booking, this is simply False Advertising!
Requested partial refund for the inconvenience but rude management didn’t want to hear about it..
Also not toilet paper or hot water in Male amenities.
As usual great time great place to stay
Great family friendly space and great for the kids as they loved the water slides and the pool

Great place to stay. Well-equipped camp kitchen and lots of things for kids to do. Water slides are awesome. Will be back again for sure. One thing to keep in mind when planning a visit is that ensuite sites are quite small if you have a lot of equipment.
All good
Great place to take the family
Great park for the kid's

Not set near the beach. A 15 min walk or 5min drive. Can walk into town, about 15 min. Excellent Heydens pie shop nearby.
Staff were very friendly and helpful.
The ensuite sites have slabs but also dirt not lawn. Our tent inside, tables, chairs everything was constantly covered in grime. When it rained the tent was very dirty. The ground is very hard so bring strong pegs.
Amenities are excellent. Playground and water sleds are excellent for the children.
The jacuzzi and sauna were constantly occupied, I think a booking system would be great.
The pool is a fair walk from the Camp sites as it is set amongst the cabins.
The campground is set between 2 major roads, so there is constant traffic noise. And a few times we heard some doing doughnuts on the gravel section off the side road.
The ensuites were really good and a great comfort to have your own, however, depending on your preference, they are situated right by the kids entertainment area.
All the games in the games room are coin operated except for the ping pong table.
Camp kitchen, laundry, gym, BBQs are all excellent.
The non-ensuite sites are grassed so not so dusty and messy.
Overall our stay was good.
They sell ice. Have a tennis court.
Enjoy your stay.
Best!! Love this Place & so do the kiddies!!
lovely set up, amazing equipment for the kids, cabin was the perfect set up for us. Would recommend, we prefer begin closer to the beach but we knew that when booking.

Fantastic facilities and close to fantastic beaches and the Ulladulla town centre.

We used the ensuite site for our little camping set up and it was perfect for me and my young family.
Please make sure you get a email confirmation as my booking my cancelled but I had banking details that I paid, lots of messing around and stress before a family funeral to get it sorted out.

We have just finished a three night stay here in a two bedroom cottage with our grandson and we’re super impressed. On line information and photos are all up to date and on line booking was very easy; Reception and check in was equally easy and very friendly.|The two bedroom with open kitchen, dining and lounge is quite large enough as are the two bedrooms, bathroom and front deck. |Beds are very comfortable, linen is high quality. Kitchen is well set out, full size fridge and good selection of cooking utensils, crockery & cutlery. Dining & lounge furniture is very comfortable and good quality.|Easy walking distance to bbq, pool, reception, spa & sauna, library, and adventure/water slide games area.|All facilities are well maintained and exceptionally clean.|Our grandson (6yo) was super impressed and definitely had a great stay 😊
Great park, great facilities for kids, great set up for all
Very disappointed ! Heated pool not working,  toilets bin always full and of course all the renaming trash is on the ground

We booked for 5nights in a cabin paid upfront.  1st night great, then a group of familys turned up, noisy, dirty, no consideration for anybody, the bbq area was filfy as they never cleaned it, blairing music most of the night, there kids swearing late at night,. Told reception, they just said sorry we cant do anything, so we packed up our cabin and moved caravan parks , no refund just credit. So far we dont have. What was surpose to be a nice 5 night away turned out horrible,  we will  never go back there. We have stayed in hundreds of caravan parks in Australia and Tasmania sorry but your park was the worse as nothing done about the other families. I have taken photos of the bbq area but have no were to post them . Last Igenia holiday park we will ever stay, sticking back to big4 and discovery parks, never ever had a drama in those parks anywhere in Australia.most parks are strict when it comes to behaviour,  but they were allowed to carry on. You even shut the slide and swimming pool because of them, so what about the rest of us?  So then they all went into the spar and sauna area, disregarding the shower before you go in  and all that was inside were letting them in the exit door. There were probably 15 of them in there noisy as, so again everybody else missed out. They should have been thrown out. Thank you we received our credit.

We just had a lovely stay here for a few days with our van. Overall we were happy and the few staff members we encountered were really friendly and the location is great. Problem is there is not enough staff and the amenities were quite dirty. One pool was full of duck poo and the pool near the slides was a little gross so we didn’t swim. Toilet blocks were a little gross too with dirty toilets and lots of toilet paper on the ground. Would be happy to return if they had more staff on board to clean more regularly especially during peak times such as school holidays.

Good little caravan Park. The only thing I didn't like was that the ensuite sites are fairly closely packed in. Some of the other users were smoking and the people behind us were really loud and up late.

We stayed at Ingenia while attending a local dance competition and found the park to great for it facilities and team spirit! The water park if great for the kids and the BBQ areas were fantastic for us to have team building sessions at the end of the day.
Nice place to relax and also fun place for kids..

If you are looking for an all-rounder family holiday spot - this is the place for you! Fun for the kids with the slides, pools and playgrounds, relaxing for the adults with the spas and aircon. Just down the road from Ulladulla CBD and short drive from Batemans Bay. Lovely staff always willing to assist. Spacious cabins with all home essentials.
Ingenia Holidays Uladulla was easy to find excellent facilities and spotlessly clean.
I loved the sauna and the spa.

I had made a booking for two nights and unfortunately became unwell and required emergency surgery just a few days before we were meant to go away and spent serval nights in hospital. After still being unable to get out of bed, let alone walk I had phoned to cancel the booking, however, despite receiving emergency surgery they would not budge on the $50 cancelation fee, which is quite a lot of money, despite cancelling a few days in advance. I understand the policy however given the circumstances I feel as though this was incredibly poor on their behalf. Will never stay here nor recommend to anyone.
We absolutely loved our stay ! Perfect location. Very clean amenities. The staff were all very Friendly. I would highly recommend this place👍
It is a very friendly park. Amenities are very clean and grounds are well maintained. Good sized sites
Great facilities and the staff member at reception was very helpful and friendly.
Very helpful and friendly staff, very clean amenities and the park in general. Some great areas for the kids. Never seen a better camp kitchen.

Absolutely perfect cabin.! Our bed is divine..! What a beautiful Park.
The staff member - Jodi - was so accommodating, absolutely lovely.! Definitely a few days R&R 🥰
Great customer service from mason
Fabulous clean friendly staff love it!!
The service was excellent and the park was perfectly maintained.  We'd definitely stay here again.   Maybe provide some toiletries for the ensuite sites though.
